items.label: Development Complete — Schemas
messages: Now I have full context. Let me implement the schema changes.  **Intent: Implementing HealthResponseSchema for health-badge feature**
Good - this is a purely additive change (new file + barrel export update). No blast radius concerns. Let me create the schema and tests now.
Now let me add the tests for the new schema:
Now let me build and test:
All 38 tests pass (including 8 new HealthResponseSchema tests). Now let me run the post-change review:

Review confirms: **no breaking changes**, **minimal structural impact** (1.8% footprint), **zero new issues**, health score unchanged. This is a clean additive change.  Now let me commit and mark completion:

**Schema-dev phase complete.** Here's a summary of what was delivered:  ### Changes Made  1. **`packages/schemas/src/health.ts`** (new file)    - `HealthResponseSchema` — Zod object with `status: z.literal("ok")` and `timestamp: z.string().datetime()`    - `HealthResponse` type derived via `z.infer<>`  2. **`packages/schemas/src/index.ts`** (updated)    - Added barrel exports for `HealthResponseSchema` and `HealthResponse` type  3. **`packages/schemas/src/__tests__/schemas.test.ts`** (updated)    - 8 new test cases for `HealthResponseSchema`: valid parse, timestamp variants, literal enforcement, missing fields, invalid timestamp, empty object, extra field stripping  ### Verification - ✅ `npm run build` — compiles cleanly - ✅ `npx jest --verbose` — all 38 tests pass (30 existing + 8 new) - ✅ `roam_review_change` — no breaking changes, minimal footprint (1.8%), zero new issues

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/packages/schemas && npm run build 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/packages/schemas && npx jest --verbose 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh backend "feat(schemas): add HealthResponseSchema for health-badge feature" 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete health-badge schema-dev 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh pipeline "chore(pipeline): mark Development Complete — Schemas" 2>&1

items.label: Infrastructure Written — Terraform
messages: Now I have a complete understanding of the infrastructure. Let me analyze:  **Spec requirements for infra:** 1. Update `infra/api-specs/api-sample.openapi.yaml` — add `GET /health` path with 200 response schema 2. The health endpoint is `authLevel: "anonymous"` — must work without credentials 3. The Sample API has a blanket auth policy (check-header / validate-jwt) — `/health` needs a passthrough exception  Let me check the existing schemas for the data contract reference:

Good — the schema is already created by another agent. The contract is clear: `{ status: "ok", timestamp: "<ISO-8601>" }`.  Now let me implement the two infrastructure changes:  1. **Update `api-sample.openapi.yaml`** — add `GET /health` path 2. **Add operation-level policy in `apim.tf`** — bypass auth for the health operation  The Sample API has a blanket auth policy applied at the API level. Since `/health` is anonymous, I need an operation-level policy that does NOT inherit the API-level auth check (no `<base />` in inbound).
Now add the operation-level policy in `apim.tf` to bypass auth for the `/health` endpoint. I'll add it right after the API-level policy for the Sample API:
Now let me verify the files look correct:
Both files look good. Now let me run the Terraform validation gate:

Terraform plan succeeded: **1 resource to add** (`azurerm_api_management_api_operation_policy.health`), 10 existing resources to update (the OpenAPI spec change triggers the API resource update). No destroys.  Now let me commit and complete:

All done. Here's a summary of what was implemented:  ## Infrastructure Changes — health-badge  ### What was done  1. **`infra/api-specs/api-sample.openapi.yaml`** — Added `GET /health` path with:    - `operationId: health`    - 200 response schema matching the `HealthResponseSchema` contract: `{ status: "ok" (enum), timestamp: date-time }`    - No auth parameters (anonymous endpoint)  2. **`infra/apim.tf`** — Added `azurerm_api_management_api_operation_policy.health` (section 6a):    - **Operation-level APIM policy** that bypasses the API-level auth check    - Omits `<base />` in `<inbound>` to prevent inheriting `check-header` (demo) / `validate-jwt` (entra) from the Sample API policy    - Explicitly includes CORS (same origins as the API policy) and `set-backend-service` for routing to the Function App    - Only allows `GET` and `OPTIONS` methods  ### Key Design Decision The Sample API applies a blanket auth policy at the API level. Since `/health` must be anonymous (`authLevel: "anonymous"`), the operation-level policy skips policy inheritance by omitting `<base />` in inbound, while still providing CORS and backend routing explicitly.  ### Terraform Plan Result - ✅ **1 to add:** `azurerm_api_management_api_operation_policy.health` - ✅ **10 to change** (OpenAPI spec update propagates to existing APIM resources) - ✅ **0 to destroy**

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/infra && az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✅ Azure CLI authenticated" || echo "❌ Not authenticated"
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/infra && terraform init -input=false 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/infra && terraform validate 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/infra && terraform plan -var-file=dev.tfvars -out=tfplan -no-color 2>&1 | tee plan-output.txt
tail -30 /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/infra/plan-output.txt
grep -A2 "will be created\|to add" /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/infra/plan-output.txt | head -20
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh infra "feat(infra): add GET /health to OpenAPI spec and APIM anonymous passthrough policy"
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:doc-note health-badge infra-architect "## Infrastructure Changes (health-badge)
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete health-badge infra-architect
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh pipeline "chore(pipeline): mark Infrastructure Written — Terraform"

items.label: Draft PR Created
messages: Good, I have all the information I need. No existing PR found, I'm on the `feature/health-badge` branch, and the spec describes a "System Health Badge" feature. Let me create the draft PR.
Draft PR #28 created successfully. Now let me clean up and update the pipeline state.

shellCommands.command: cat /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/in-progress/health-badge_SPEC.md | head -20
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && EXISTING_PR=$(gh pr list --head "feature/health-badge" --json number -q '.[0].number' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && git branch --show-current && git remote -v | head -2
cat /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/in-progress/system-health-badge_SPEC.md | head -30
cat > /workspaces/DAGent-t/DRAFT_PR_BODY.md << 'EOF'
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && PR_URL=$(gh pr create \
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && rm -f DRAFT_PR_BODY.md && echo "Cleaned up body file"
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete health-badge create-draft-pr 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:set-url health-badge "https://github.com/RomanKaliupinMelonusa/DAGent-t/pull/28" 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:set-note health-badge "Draft PR #28 created — awaiting Terraform plan" 2>&1
